title: morella brevifolia
intro: Morella brevifolia, also known as the coastal red bush, is a shrub native to the eastern coast of Australia. This plant has been traditionally used by the Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The coastal red bush has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and branches of the coastal red bush can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
